metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fantastic weekend filled with fun activities! I've found some amazing events in and around Hackney, including puppet shows, interactive museum exhibits, and outdoor adventures perfect for a curious 3-year-old. Let's dive into the excitement!

activities.title: Tiny Planet
activities.address: 43 White Horse Road, Limehouse, London E1 0ND
activities.description: A children's theatre show at the Half Moon Theatre, located in Limehouse, East London. Half Moon produces and presents professional theatre for children at their venue, as well as touring work nationally.
activities.whyItWorks: While slightly above the target age, the affordable price and engaging performances make it worth considering. The theatre is local, making it easily accessible.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes and DLR services are available from Hackney to Limehouse. Check Citymapper for the best route.
transport.tip: Consider using public transport to avoid parking issues in Limehouse.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Explore the newly transformed Young V&A, co-designed with children. It features three permanent gallery spaces: Imagine, Play, and Design. Get hands-on with interactive displays and workshops.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry, interactive exhibits, and spaces designed specifically for children make this a perfect option for a 3-year-old. The 'Play' area is particularly suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or Overground to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from Bethnal Green tube station (Central line).
transport.tip: Check for special events that might interest your child.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for giant Easter eggs hidden around the park. Enjoy seeing giraffes, lions, monkeys, and more incredible wildlife.
activities.whyItWorks: The combination of animals and an Easter egg hunt is perfect for a 3-year-old. The zoo provides plenty of space to move around.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Euston, then the tube to Regent's Park. Or take a bus directly to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.address: King William Walk, Greenwich, London SE10 9HT
activities.description: Step aboard the Cutty Sark in Greenwich and take part in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover fascinating facts and unscramble a secret nautical code.
activities.whyItWorks: While best suited for slightly older children, the novelty of exploring a ship and the Easter egg hunt can be engaging for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Greenwich or a train to Greenwich station. The Cutty Sark is a short walk from both.
transport.tip: Combine this with a visit to Greenwich Park for a full day out.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Walk through the gardens, looking for Lindt Gold Bunny statues peeking out from daffodils. There's a chocolate prize at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: The outdoor hunt and promise of chocolate are great motivators for little ones. The Magic Garden playground at Hampton Court Palace is also fantastic.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train from Liverpool Street Station to Hampton Court. It's about an hour's journey.
transport.tip: Pack snacks and drinks for the train journey.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: A world-famous arts centre with a dynamic year-round program. Check for family events, including free concerts, DJ sets, and interactive activities. REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground runs until 12 Apr 2026.
activities.whyItWorks: The Southbank Centre offers a wide range of activities, many of which are free. The open spaces and variety of entertainment make it suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for specific family-friendly events.

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: A stage adaptation of Julia Donaldson's story brought to life with rhyme, original music, and expressive puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppet shows are highly engaging for toddlers. The Little Angel Theatre is a dedicated puppet theatre with shows tailored for this age group.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Angel or Highbury & Islington, then a short walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend shows.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.address: 147 Tooley Street, London Bridge, London, SE1 2HZ
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive theatre experience where children can engage with the performance by lending a toy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to London Bridge.
transport.tip: Check show times and book in advance as this sounds very popular!

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: Everyone's favourite Peruvian import gets the West End treatment.  It uses music and choreography to tell the story of the bear's arrival at Paddington station, and how he adapts to London life.
activities.whyItWorks: Paddington is a well-loved character, and this musical is designed to be engaging for children. Note that it's geared toward slightly older children, so consider your child's attention span.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to Covent Garden or Charing Cross.
transport.tip: Consider aisle seats for a good view.
